core_mechanism: Construction of configurations by quotienting a space by a discrete symmetry group (an orbifold-type quotient) so that a residual discrete symmetry survives and group-theoretic selection rules fix which couplings are allowed, with the resulting discrete ensemble scanned for members that satisfy a set of target constraints.
brief_reason: The abstract builds models on a Z6-II orbifold (a quotient of a space by a discrete symmetry group), retains an exact R parity (a residual discrete symmetry surviving the quotient), invokes 'string selection rules' and the 'gamma-rule' to control which couplings are allowed, and searches a 'landscape' of such models for those matching the desired spectrum and flat-direction decoupling of unwanted modes.
